video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network

restaurants.name: T-Bone Tom's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm south of Houston, almost by Galveston, in a little town called Kima. But there's also this great little eatery here, T-Bone Tom's, where I hear these folks have been hand-cutting their steaks for over 35 years.

dishes.description: Marinated ribeye steak, hand-cut and charbroiled to medium-rare perfection.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm, that's a great steak. That really is. Wow, that's a nice piece of meat.

dishes.description: House-made Polish sausage, 50% beef and 50% pork, stuffed and smoked on the pit.
dishes.guy_response: Hear the little snap of that casing? And look at the juice coming out of that. Mmm. That is about as tender a sausage I've ever tried.

dishes.description: Deep-fried pickled jalapenos stuffed with smoked brisket and homemade barbecue sauce, double-dredged in flour, buttermilk, and panko.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Oh, yeah. Wow. I mean, the barbecue's great. The jalapeno being a pickled jalapeno really makes it. That's killer. I mean, killer, killer.

restaurants.name: Bob's Taco Station and Snowballs
restaurants.guy_intro: All you got to do is slow down and take a look around, like here in Rosenberg, Texas, 30 miles southwest of Houston, where an abandoned gas station has morphed into Bob's Taco Station and Snowballs.

dishes.description: Cubed blade meat cooked with red peppers, onion, green bell pepper, and tomato, seasoned with a spice mix, served on a homemade flour tortilla.
dishes.guy_response: The meat, super tender. Got some creaminess to it. I love seeing the peppers inside of it.

dishes.description: Pork butt simmered until tender, shredded, mixed with lard, pureed garlic, cumin, pepper, and guajillo pepper, then wrapped in masa and corn husks, steamed.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Delicious. That pork. Nice, moist, right amount of seasoning. I like the meat ratio.

dishes.description: Finely shaved ice flavored with Dreamsicle syrup and cream, served like a snow cone.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, this is nuts. It tastes just like a 50-50 bar. This is nothing like a snow cone.

restaurants.name: 900 Grayson
restaurants.guy_intro: We're going to start off with a little joint out of Berkeley called 900 Grayson.

dishes.dish_name: Ladyboy Thai Noodle Salad with Grilled Shrimp
dishes.description: Rice noodles with mango, zucchini, carrot, cucumber, daikon, mint, and basil, tossed in a dressing, topped with grilled marinated shrimp, sriracha aioli, rice powder, chili oil, and basil oil.
dishes.guy_response: The basil oil and the chili. Chili oil are right there. But you know what really is the kicker for me on that? That toasted rice. Oh, that's good.

dishes.description: Fried chicken breast served on a buttermilk waffle, topped with a creamy vegetarian gravy and Aleppo pepper.
dishes.guy_response: Savory. You get the salt and the crunch of the chicken. Then you get into the waffle. OK, this is going to be sweet. But then you. You nail it with that gravy, dude. That is a wham bam combo.

restaurants.name: Taste of Peru
restaurants.guy_intro: I think it's time that I took some great advice from my friend Maria and try some Peruvian food. And that brings me here to Taste of Peru.

dishes.description: Masa dough made with corn and rice flour, flavored with achiote oil, onion, garlic paste, chili paste, chicken bouillon, pepper, salt, and chicken stock. Filled with chicken, hot pepper, peanuts, and olive, then wrapped in banana leaf and foil, and boiled.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, that's awesome. It's the best masa I've ever tried. I think that's because of the rice flour.

dishes.description: Ribeye strips seasoned with pepper, salt, garlic, and soy sauce, stir-fried in a special Peruvian pan with onion, tomatoes, vinegar, and beer. Served with french fries and rice.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, humbucker. Fantastic. Oh, my gosh. The way the pan was so hot, you broke the tomato down so fast. So it works into the sauce, but it's not a tomato sauce.

restaurants.name: South 21
restaurants.guy_intro: We're here in Charlotte, North Carolina to check out a drive-in. This is South 21, serving Charlotte since 1955.

dishes.description: Classic burger patty served on a toasted bun with mustard, slaw, scratch-made chili, and a pickle.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. That's great. That has all of the key pieces that a great burger needs to have. The cool slaw, the bite of the onion, and then you get the chili with a little bit of spice. That is burger 101 right there, baby.

dishes.description: Freshly cut onion rings, with centers removed, double-breaded in a milk/egg/flour batter and non-salted cracker meal, then deep-fried until golden brown.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Really nice onion. The batter's nice and crunchy. I want some salt on it. Little ketchup. Just like I said about the Superboy, that right there is onion ring 101.

restaurants.name: Betty's Ocean View Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Berkeley, California, to check out Betty's Ocean View Diner.

dishes.description: Pork hocks and pork butt cooked to make a stock, meat shredded and ground, then mixed with stock, cayenne, sage, black pepper, white and yellow cornmeal. Chilled, sliced, and pan-fried in clarified butter. Served with grilled tomatoes and homemade applesauce.
dishes.guy_response: If that's what scrapple tasted like everywhere across the country, I'd eat scrapple every breakfast. The crunch on this is crunch-tastic.

dishes.description: Fresh rock cod dredged in seasoned flour (salt, pepper, Old Bay), pan-fried in clarified butter. Served on a toasted baguette with lettuce and homemade tartar sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Now that's how you're supposed to do it. Taste the fish. A really nice, complimentary tartar sauce that doesn't steal all the thunder.

dishes.description: Batter made with egg yolks, sugar, salt, clarified butter, half and half, and flour. Egg whites are folded in per order, then pan-fried in clarified butter and finished in the oven.
dishes.guy_response: Now see, this is nice and light and airy. This is like a pancake meets a light English muffin. Good flavor, nice texture on the top.

restaurants.name: Dish
restaurants.guy_intro: These folks are serving up some Southern classics. This is Dish.

dishes.description: Whole chicken roasted and shredded. Stock made from bones, skin, white onion, pepper, celery salt, granulated garlic, and onion. Dumplings made from flour, eggs, and melted butter, cooked in the stock and thickened with a roux.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Awesome. Really awesome. We got a super tender dumpling. And I like the fact that you roasted the chicken, so it's got real chicken flavor. That is down home chicken and dumplings.

dishes.description: Fresh salmon diced and mixed with parsley, pulverized onions, whisked eggs, mustard, mayonnaise, celery, lemon juice, lemon zest, dill weed, and white breadcrumbs. Formed into patties, rolled in panko, and pan-fried. Served with honey orange butter.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That's nice. Dill right off the bat. Salmon comes in. There's a lot of crunch on that cake. It's just really nice. The nice little touch of adding the butter brings it all together.

restaurants.name: Aldo's
restaurants.guy_intro: Today we're on the water in Santa Cruz, California, to check out a third-generation seafood restaurant that started as a bait and tackle shop. It's time to dock up to Aldo's.

dishes.description: Homemade pasta dough filled with a mixture of boiled spinach and Swiss chard, focaccia breadcrumbs, sautéed onions, Romano and Parmesan cheeses, eggs, and olive oil. Served with marinara sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Awesome. If there's a time that I really want fresh pasta, it's when it comes in a ravioli. The two cheeses, the Swiss chard. That's impressive.

dishes.description: Seafood stew with rock cod, shrimp, mussels, clams, calamari, and crab legs, cooked in a marinara sauce with butter, chili pepper, garlic, capers, and white wine.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Everything is nice and tender. You can taste the seafood inside of the sauce. Great flavor. It's all around. It's really spicy.

restaurants.name: Landmark Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, here's the oasis in Charlotte, North Carolina. Landmark diner.

dishes.description: Turkey boiled with onions, celery, carrots, pepper, salt, and water. Served with gravy made from turkey stock, butter, flour, salt, and black pepper. Stuffing made with vegetable oil, onions, celery, carrots, butter, apple mixture, garlic, sage, black pepper, salt, sugar, poultry seasoning, challah bread, and eggs.
dishes.guy_response: Hm. Wow, that's big flavor, man. Tender turkey, big flavor in the gravy. I tell you, man, it's good. That's Thanksgiving on a plate every day.

dishes.description: Sautéed white onions, green onions, and dill mixed with eggs, spinach, and feta cheese. Layered between filo dough sheets brushed with olive oil and butter, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: That's great. The dill. The dill actually really makes that. It complements really nice with the feta. That's good, dude. It's simple, done right.

restaurants.name: Highway Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: Starting off with old school Hawaiian cooking at a place called Highway Inn.

dishes.description: Pork belly and salted butterfish wrapped in taro leaves and tea leaves, tied, then steamed for two hours. Served with poi.
dishes.guy_response: It is much, much better. Mm. That's good. The fat really mellows it out. And you got a little bit amount of salt in there. I love it.

dishes.description: Chuck roll sliced, marinated with salt and shoyu (soy sauce), then hung above stoves to dry for four hours. Pan-fried on a flat top and chopped.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Wow. Kind of reminds me of dry aged beef. A little saltier. And it kind of gives me a feeling of like wet beef jerky. The richness of it. It's deep flavor.
